title: tasks-mux: missing CLI commands and MCP tools
acceptanceCriteria: The implementation starts with a reuse audit and contract/spec phase before production changes.
Shared backend/type contracts support the required task lifecycle operations or report explicit unsupported-backend errors.
The CLI exposes the requested breakpoints, responders, templates, and rules commands with JSON and table output coverage.
The MCP server exposes the requested tools with schema, handler, backend resolution, and transport coverage.

The MCP server exposes breakpoint://[id] resources and state-change subscriptions where supported by the current MCP SDK, with explicit documented fallback behavior if a transport cannot support subscriptions.
Existing CLI and MCP commands remain backward compatible.
Contract tests fail before implementation and pass after implementation.
Docs and package metadata match the implemented public surface.
explicitNonGoals: Do not wire tasks-mux MCP tools into agent-platform or agent-core discovery unless required by issue #597; that is related to #577.

Do not implement unrelated task priority, dependency, notification provider, database backend, Slack, Linear, or Jira support unless it is necessary to expose an explicit unsupported capability boundary.
Do not replace the existing BreakpointBackend architecture.
qualityGates: Focused RED proof for CLI, MCP, resource, subscription, and backend contract tests before implementation.
Green focused contract tests after implementation.
Packaged public surface and metadata verification pass.
Final adversarial review approves CLI/MCP/backend parity and backward compatibility.
